meaning: adj.猖獗的,蔓生的,猛烈的,狂暴的,跛拱的
examples: By the 1920s, the weed was rampant.
Malaria is still rampant in some swampy regions.
Inflation is rampant and industry in decline.
Part of that is simply by getting rid of inefficiency that is rampant in places like 10B.
Rampant logging, which has devastated the environment, has slowed because of the regional economic crisis.
